ogTitle: Old Station vs Old Toongabbie: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Old Station and Old Toongabbie.
heading: Old Station vs Old Toongabbie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Station, New South Wales and Old Toongabbie, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Old Toongabbie has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Old Station (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Station or Old Toongabbie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Station has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Old Toongabbie scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Station scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Old Toongabbie scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Station or Old Toongabbie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Old Toongabbie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
